heading: Tax treatment of stripped bonds
label: 1286 Tax treatment of stripped bonds
notes: References in TextSection 1272(d), referred to in subsec. (d)(6), was redesignated section 1272(c) by , , .Pub. L. 115–141, div. U, title IV, § 401(c)(1)(B)132 Stat. 1205Mar. 23, 2018

Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). , struck out “after ,” before “a stripped bond” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(c)(2)(B)July 1, 1982Subsec. (b). , struck out “after ,” before “disposes of the bond” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(c)(2)(B)July 1, 1982Subsec. (c). , redesignated subsec. (d) as (c) and struck out former subsec. (c) which related to retention of existing law for stripped bonds purchased before .Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(c)(2)(A)July 2, 1982Subsec. (d). , redesignated subsec. (e) as (d). Former subsec. (d) redesignated (c).Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(c)(2)(A)Subsec. (d)(5). , struck out at end “This paragraph shall apply for purposes of subsection (c) only in the case of purchases after .”Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(c)(2)(C)July 1, 1982Subsec. (e). , redesignated subsec. (f) as (e). Former subsec. (e) redesignated (d).Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(c)(2)(A)Subsec. (f). , redesignated subsec. (g) as (f). Former subsec. (f) redesignated (e).Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(c)(2)(A), substituted “and section 305(e),” for “and 305(e),”.Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(a)(182)Subsec. (g). , redesignated subsec. (g) as (f).Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(c)(2)(A)2004—Subsecs. (f), (g). added subsec. (f) and redesignated former subsec. (f) as (g).Pub. L. 108–3571988—Subsec. (d). amended subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (d) read as follows: “In the case of any tax-exempt obligation (as defined in section 1275(a)(3)) from which 1 or more coupons have been stripped—Pub. L. 100–647“(1) the amount of original issue discount determined under subsection (a) with respect to any stripped bond or stripped coupon from such obligation shall be the amount which produces a yield to maturity (as of the purchase date) equal to the lower of—“(A) the coupon rate of interest on such obligation before the separation of coupons, or“(B) the yield to maturity (on the basis of purchase price) of the stripped obligation or coupon,“(2) the amount of original issue discount determined under paragraph (1) shall be taken into account in determining the adjusted basis of the holder under section 1288,“(3) subsection (b)(1) shall not apply, and“(4) subsection (b)(2) shall be applied by increasing the basis of the bond or coupon by the interest accrued but not paid before the time such bond or coupon was disposed of (and not previously reflected in basis).”1986—Subsec. (b)(1). , amended par. (1) generally, designating existing provisions as subpar. (A) and adding subpar. (B).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1803(a)(13)(B)(i)Subsec. (b)(2). , substituted “the amount included in gross income under paragraph (1)” for “the amount of the accrued interest described in paragraph (1)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1803(a)(13)(B)(ii)Subsec. (d). , amended subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (d) read as follows: “In the case of any tax-exempt obligation (as defined in section 1275(a)(3))—Pub. L. 99–514, § 1879(s)(1)“(1) subsections (a) and (b)(1) shall not apply,“(2) the rules of subsection (b)(4) shall apply for purposes of subsection (c), and“(3) subsection (c) shall be applied without regard to the requirement that the bond be purchased before .”July 2, 1982

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by section 401(c)(2)(A)–(C) of applicable to bonds purchased on or after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 115–141section 401(c)(2)(E) of Pub. L. 115–141section 305 of this titleJuly 2, 1982

Effective Date of 2004 AmendmentAmendment by applicable to purchases and dispositions after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 108–357section 831(c) of Pub. L. 108–357section 305 of this titleOct. 22, 2004

Effective Date of 1988 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–647, title I, § 1018(q)(4)(B)102 Stat. 3586Nov. 10, 1988“(i)Except as provided in clause (ii), the amendment made by subparagraph (A) [amending this section] shall apply to any purchase or sale after , of any stripped tax-exempt obligation or stripped coupon from such an obligation.June 10, 1987“(ii)If—“(I)any person held any obligation or coupon in stripped form on , andJune 10, 1987“(II)such obligation or coupon was held by such person on such date for sale in the ordinary course of such person’s trade or business,the amendment made by subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any sale of such obligation or coupon by such person and shall not apply to any such obligation or coupon while held by another person who purchased such obligation or coupon from the person referred to in subclause (I).”

Effective Date of 1986 AmendmentAmendment by applicable to obligations acquired after , see , set out as a note under .section 1803(a)(13)(B) of Pub. L. 99–514section 1803(a)(13)(C) of Pub. L. 99–514section 1276 of this titleOct. 22, 1986, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–514, title XVIII, § 1879(s)(2)100 Stat. 2913Oct. 22, 1986“The amendment made by paragraph (1) [amending this section] shall apply to any purchase or sale of any stripped tax-exempt obligation or stripped coupon from such an obligation after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Oct. 22, 1986

children.heading: Inclusion in income as if bond and coupons were original issue discount bonds
children.label: a Inclusion in income as if bond and coupons were original issue discount bonds

children.text: the stated redemption price at maturity (or, in the case of coupon, the amount payable on the due date of such coupon), over
such bond’s or coupon’s ratable share of the purchase price.

children.heading: Tax treatment of person stripping bond
children.label: b Tax treatment of person stripping bond
A
children.text: the interest accrued on such bond while held by such person and before the time such coupon or bond was disposed of (to the extent such interest has not theretofore been included in such person’s gross income), and

children.label: B
children.text: the accrued market discount on such bond determined as of the time such coupon or bond was disposed of (to the extent such discount has not theretofore been included in such person’s gross income),
the basis of the bond and coupons shall be increased by the amount included in gross income under paragraph (1),

the basis of the bond and coupons immediately before the disposition (as adjusted pursuant to paragraph (2)) shall be allocated among the items retained by such person and the items disposed of by such person on the basis of their respective fair market values, and

for purposes of subsection (a), such person shall be treated as having purchased on the date of such disposition each such item which he retains for an amount equal to the basis allocated to such item under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Special rules for tax-exempt obligations
children.label: c Special rules for tax-exempt obligations
i
children.text: shall be treated as original issue discount on a tax-exempt obligation to the extent such discount does not exceed the tax-exempt portion of such discount, and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall be treated as original issue discount on an obligation which is not a tax-exempt obligation to the extent such discount exceeds the tax-exempt portion of such discount,

children.label: B
children.text: subsection (b)(1)(A) shall not apply, and

children.label: i
children.text: the interest accrued but not paid before such bond or coupon was disposed of (and not previously reflected in basis), plus

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount included in gross income under subsection (b)(1)(B).

children.label: A
children.text: the amount referred to in subsection (a)(1), over

children.label: i
children.text: the coupon rate of interest on the obligation from which the coupons were separated, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the yield to maturity (on the basis of the purchase price) of the stripped obligation or coupon.

children.heading: Bond
children.label: 1 Bond
children.text: The term “bond” means a bond, debenture, note, or certificate or other evidence of indebtedness.

children.heading: Stripped bond
children.label: 2 Stripped bond
children.text: The term “stripped bond” means a bond issued at any time with interest coupons where there is a separation in ownership between the bond and any coupon which has not yet become payable.

children.heading: Stripped coupon
children.label: 3 Stripped coupon
children.text: The term “stripped coupon” means any coupon relating to a stripped bond.

children.heading: Stated redemption price at maturity
children.label: 4 Stated redemption price at maturity
children.text: The term “stated redemption price at maturity” has the meaning given such term by section 1273(a)(2).

children.heading: Coupon
children.label: 5 Coupon
children.text: The term “coupon” includes any right to receive interest on a bond (whether or not evidenced by a coupon).

children.heading: Purchase
children.label: 6 Purchase
children.text: The term “purchase” has the meaning given such term by section 1272(d)(1).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Treatment of stripped interests in bond and preferred stock funds, etc.
children.label: e Treatment of stripped interests in bond and preferred stock funds, etc.
children.text: In the case of an account or entity substantially all of the assets of which consist of bonds, preferred stock, or a combination thereof, the Secretary may by regulations provide that rules similar to the rules of this section and section 305(e), as appropriate, shall apply to interests in such account or entity to which (but for this subsection) this section or section 305(e), as the case may be, would not apply.

children.heading: Regulation authority
children.label: f Regulation authority
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe regulations providing that where, by reason of varying rates of interest, put or call options, or other circumstances, the tax treatment under this section does not accurately reflect the income of the holder of a stripped coupon or stripped bond, or of the person disposing of such bond or coupon, as the case may be, for any period, such treatment shall be modified to require that the proper amount of income be included for such period.
